narrative.headline: 2022 Hyundai Kona: engine leads the owner complaints
narrative.summary: Owners filed 94 complaints with NHTSA. The engine is the most reported area at 33%, followed by the electrical system (22%) and power train (16%). No fires or deaths are reported. The top three areas have no crashes or injuries, but forward collision avoidance and the brakes each account for 1 crash and 1 injury, so both systems are worth a close look on a test drive.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: The most reported area: 31 reports (33%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. The largest single source of owner complaints on this car.

issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: Second most reported: 21 reports (22%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. A common but lower-severity complaint area.

issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 15 reports (16%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries. Watch for shifting problems during a test drive.

issues.title: Forward Collision Avoidance
issues.blurb: 14 reports (15%), tied to 1 crash and 1 injury. Test the cruise control and collision-avoidance features carefully.

issues.title: Service Brakes
issues.blurb: The fewest reports at 9 (10%), but tied to 1 crash and 1 injury. Test the brakes thoroughly before buying.
